resources.string: ✅ Correct! Now for the next step.
❌ Try again! Focus on the hint.
Welcome to the Vedic Path. I will guide you through each Sutra step-by-step. Solve each part to master the complete technique!
Vedic math is easier when broken down. We\'ll solve big problems in small, simple steps.
The first few problems are Demos. I\'ll give you hints for every step to get you started!
Enter the result of each step in the box below. Let\'s begin!
Sutra 4 (Paravartya) uses \'Transpose and Apply\'.\nFor divisor %1$d (Base 10), what is the transposed digit (surplus with opposite sign)?
Step 1: The first digit of the quotient is simply the first digit of the dividend (%1$d). Enter %1$d
Step 2: Multiply the quotient digit (%1$d) by the transposed digit (%2$d) and add to the next digit (%3$d).\n(%1$d × %2$d) + %3$d = ?
Step 3: This result (%1$d) is our next quotient digit. Multiply it by %2$d and add to the last digit (%3$d) to find the remainder.\n(%1$d × %2$d) + %3$d = ?
Final Step: The quotient digits are %1$d and %2$d.\nWhat is the final Quotient?
(Note: Result may be negative. Use \'-\' sign if needed)
Step 1: Find the deficiency from 100. Subtract it from the number itself (%1$d - %2$d). Result?
Step 3: Append the squared deficiency to the first result. (If square is single digit, add a leading zero). Final Answer?
Verification: Calculate the Digit Sum of %1$d (Add digits until a single digit remains).
Multiply the digit sums of both numbers. What is the Digit Sum of the result?
Completion: How much does %1$d need to reach the next multiple of 10 (%2$d)?
Subtract this from %1$d and add it to %2$d. Result?
